reviews.review_description:
Update:

I'm requesting a refund. A critical interaction in the game, (covering your mouth), is extremely janky and this seriously impacts the gameplay. If I ever end up buying this again it'll be during a very substantial sale.

Original Review:

When I saw that this game finally received an update I took a chance. I really enjoyed Wales Interactive's "Don't Knock Twice" on PlayStation VR1, (once they'd fixed a game breaking bug), and I was looking forward to playing another VR horror game from them.

All the negative reviews and gameplay videos didn't prepare me for how astonishingly poor the rendering looks and performs when you're in the headset.

This ranks among the worst graphics rendering I've encountered in my ten years of using VR. Only Obduction on PlayStation VR1 and some Oculus Go games I've played are worse.

You start in an outdoor forested environment, the most taxing place for a 3D engine. It's difficult to describe just how awful this looks in words, but try to imagine Resident Evil on PlayStation 2 only with less detailed geometry, and frequent hitching, stuttering, and pausing.  The woods have what I call "cardboard trees".  Trees made of two flat drawings rotated 90 degrees to each other, like trees children make for school dioramas using crayons and cardboard. I haven't seen that modelling technique used in video games for a long while.

To make the visuals even more jarring if you turn to look around using your feet the edges of the view flicker and shimmer like the rendering engine can't keep up so it gives up and draws the edges later when it can get to it.

The beginning of the game looks so shockingly bad that I wouldn't be surprised if people request refunds less than five minutes after starting it.

The situation gets slightly better indoors because then you can freely turn without your peripheral vision flashing and flickering. Unfortunately the performance is still poor with frequent hitching and stalling as the game desperately struggles to render its PS2 era visuals.

It's astonishing that graphics cut down so drastically can't run smoothly on the Quest 3. Games like Batman, Propagation: Paradise Hotel, and Metro Awakening have far better rendering and they manage to run on the Quest 3 without hitching and stalling.

The VR interactions and interface are also primitive and indicate either a lack of VR experience or lack of care by the developers.

Interacting with buttons is strange in that you have to hover over them until a circular indicator bar fills up. It feels odd to not be able to simply press a button in VR.

You can get stuck navigating through hallways and doors and have to wiggle around to find the sweet spot that lets you pass.

If you press pause to go to the main menu it puts you floating above the ground with the environment pitched at an angle. That would be fine on a TV screen but in VR it's really jarring. If you play in roomscale and turn with your feet the game has no idea which way you're facing so sometimes you have to turn around in this pitched environment to find the menu.  Apparently the developers didn't test the game in roomscale despite claiming to support it.

It also seems that the developers didn't properly test in standing mode either since the automatic height calibration puts you at the height where in game you'd be sitting in a chair. Luckily there's a manual height adjustment.

The interface for interacting with your inventory, notes, map, etc. is just a standard 2D game menu. It's clunky but it mostly works fine although it occasionally gets glitchy if you pick up a document to read it.

I really can't imagine any amount of patching getting this game up to par. I think only deleting the entire code base and contracting the port out to a team more familiar with VR and the Quest platform could save it.

Despite all these serious technical issues I'm not going to request a refund.

Once my brain began to adapt to the dated visuals the underlying art direction started to shine through the pixelated textures and inconsistent frame rate.

The atmosphere has a Resident Evil 7 vibe that I love and the story is interesting. Once I got spooked and unnerved I knew the game had the ability to immerse me despite all of its glaring problems.

I'm probably going to shelve this for now and save it to play around Halloween.

It's a shame it was so poorly ported to the Quest 3, but it's the only place I can play it for now.

reviews.review_title: I’m struggling to keep playing. So bland
reviews.review_description:
First 30 minute reaction.

As much as I enjoyed the creepy atmosphere, I personally can’t justify the $24 price for this game. 

There’s not a single human or sign of life on the train. (I know I’m knit picking but it’s like the devs don’t have any soul in creating an immersive experience.) 

The picture texture is of low quality. There’s quite a bit of black flickering. Your hands are these laughable white transparent hands. There’s nothing you can interact with except things that are apart of the story. The frame rate is a bit jittery at times. Unfortunately there is no option to remove the vignette which further dissolves the immersive feeling. 

Your character has no voice actor and you have to annoyingly click for each chat option. A character says you’d be attacked if make any noise, but I’ve been making a racket ringing bells and playing a  piano and was not attacked. 

The only positive things I can say is that it does have a creepy vibe, it has potential for a good story and the music is surreal. But visually, interactively, and mechanically, this is definitely not worth $24 . It’s giving me PTSD experience when I played syberia at launch. And still was not enjoyable months later.

So, how can I say this… it really breaks my heart, but this game should have been delayed a bit longer.
First of all, the graphics are honestly very poor, especially for a Quest 3 title.
Paranormal Activity, which is over 6 years old, looked better in terms of graphics.
There are also a lot of FPS drops.
The game needs to be updated as soon as possible. I’m saying this with care, because I know you’re a small team. I won’t ask for a refund, and I hope the $30 will at least help support you, because small studios deserve encouragement.
I’ll wait and come back to the game if patches are released.

Update : Despite a few small issues, I've upgraded to 5 stars. This is a really good game!!! OK, it's better than some are saying. Maybe it's been patched since older reviews. A bit janky at start before getting into the game proper but it settles down. I did the 20 minutes trial which for some reason continued for an hour and when I stopped there was 7 minutes left! It did stop then and I've just bought it. Spooky puzzlers are exactly for me. One note to developers. Please give us the option of removing the vignette. I don't get motion sickness and a vignette badly affects immersion. Please change that quickly as I would prefer to continue the game without it. I want to come back and give it 5 stars, take away the mandatory vignette and you've got it! For those who tried It earlier, it might be worth trying again, particularly if they can optimize it a bit more.

Played about 1 hr, great start so far atmosphere is fantastic and super creepy with some early jumpscares. Sure graphics could be better outside but inside looks pretty good graphically and gameplay is simple and solid. Its looks to be a intense adventure winding up . More to come later.

I enjoyed it. I played the entire game before leaving a review. Which I think should be mandatory for leaving a review. I'm not big on horror games. I don't like that nervous feeling waiting to be scared. This game had a safe mode. I got to explore the place without worrying about being too scared. There were still some scary spots and a creepy vibe to it. But one i could handle. The movement was great. It had everything...seated, standing, continous, snap. There was tons to explore. Lots of things to find. Some puzzles to figure out. Was it the greatest game or greatest story ever? Or the most exciting game I've ever played? No. But it checked all the boxes of what a game like this should be. My total game time was 4 hours. I didn't even finish all the achievements or find everything. When I go back in to complete everything, it will probably be around 5 hours. I think that is worth $24.99. I didn't experience any glitches or lagging. The game was smooth and looked good. It's not like VR ever looks like real life. People need to be realistic when they leave reviews. Anyway, I personally enjoyed it. I was playing on the Quest 3s.
graphics for the quest three s is not good and there's a lot of jitterine and glitching, but I'm going to keep playing and hope it gets better

reviews.review_title: Worst game ever..I want my $ back
